activities_projects.title: Proportion Verification Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Verify proportional relationships using cross multiplication
steps: Write five pairs of ratios such as 4:5 and 16:20
Use cross multiplication to test if they are proportional
Explain why each pair is proportional or not
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies cross multiplication to verify proportions

activities_projects.title: Scale Drawing Investigation
steps: Draw a rectangle with width 4 cm and height 3 cm
Scale the rectangle by a factor of 2 and redraw it
Compare the ratios of the original and scaled rectangles
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of drawing and ratio comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ratio Pattern Investigation
steps: Choose a ratio such as 3:5
Generate at least five equivalent ratios
Explain how the ratios are related

activities_projects.title: Recipe Scaling Project
activities_projects.objective: Apply proportional reasoning to real-life cooking
steps: Write a recipe for 4 people
Adjust the quantities for 10 people
Explain how proportional reasoning was used

activities_projects.title: Distance–Time Proportion Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand proportional relationships between distance and time
steps: Assume a car travels 60 km in 1 hour
Calculate distances for 2, 3 and 5 hours
Explain how the relationship shows proportional reasoning

activities_projects.title: Proportional Rectangle Investigation
steps: Draw three rectangles with width-height ratios of 3:2
Measure the sides
Explain why the rectangles are similar

activities_projects.title: Missing Term in Proportion
steps: Write proportions like 7:9 :: x:27
Use cross multiplication to find the missing value
Verify the solution

activities_projects.title: Ratio Graph Investigation
steps: Create a table of proportional values such as (2,4), (4,8), (6,12)
Plot the points on graph paper
Observe the pattern formed

activities_projects.title: Class Data Ratio Analysis
steps: Collect data such as number of students in different classes
Write ratios between classes
Check if any ratios are proportional
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies ratios to real data analysis

activities_projects.title: Proportional Reasoning Research
steps: Research examples of proportional reasoning in daily life
Prepare a chart with at least three examples
Explain how ratios and proportions are used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands real-world applications of proportional reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of explanation (10 marks)
